Steps: Go to the portal URL (e.g. http://localhost:5173). You should see the login page.
Expected Result: Login page loads with an email input field and a branded design.

Steps: On the login page type a known customer email address and press Continue.
Expected Result: The system shows either a password field (returning user) or a 6-digit code step (new user). A loading animation appears while it checks your account.

Steps: On the login page type an email that is NOT a known customer (e.g. a personal gmail).
Expected Result: The system shows an onboarding form asking for first name / last name / phone / company OR proceeds to the code step depending on settings.

Steps: After receiving the email code enter all 6 digits in the code input.
Expected Result: You are logged in and taken to the Dashboard.

Steps: Enter a wrong 6-digit code on the verification screen.
Expected Result: An error message appears. You are NOT logged in.

Steps: On the code entry screen click Resend code.
Expected Result: The button shows a 30-second countdown and a new code is sent to your email.

Test Name: Switch to email code from password
Steps: If you see a password field click the Use email code instead link.
Expected Result: The screen switches to the 6-digit code entry step.

Steps: After verifying your code for the first time you may be asked to set a password. Enter a password (8+ characters) and confirm it.
Expected Result: Password is saved and you are logged in.

Steps: Click the user avatar / menu in the top-right and select Sign out.
Expected Result: You are returned to the login page and can no longer access portal pages.

Steps: If redirected to the onboarding page fill in first name / last name / phone / job title and click Save.
Expected Result: Your profile is saved and you are redirected to the Dashboard.

Steps: On the onboarding page leave one or more required fields blank and click Save.
Expected Result: An error or validation message appears. The form does not submit.

Steps: Log in and arrive at the Dashboard.
Expected Result: You see a welcome greeting with your name and summary cards for upcoming events / meetings / invoices.

Steps: On the Dashboard look for the pipeline stage indicator (Discovery > Demo > Contract > Training > Live).
Expected Result: The tracker is visible and your current stage is highlighted.

Steps: On the Dashboard check the upcoming events section.
Expected Result: Your next events are listed with titles and dates. Clicking one takes you to the event detail.

Steps: On the Dashboard check the next meeting card.
Expected Result: If you have an upcoming meeting you see the host name / date / time and a Join button.

Steps: Click the Contact Us button on the Dashboard.
Expected Result: A contact form modal opens where you can submit a message.

Steps: If an admin has set a global notification check the top of the page.
Expected Result: A coloured banner (info / warning / danger) appears with the admin's message.

Steps: Click Events in the sidebar.
Expected Result: A list of upcoming events appears with title / type / date for each.

Steps: On the Events page type a keyword into the search box.
Expected Result: Only events matching your search term are shown.

Steps: Click on any event in the list.
Expected Result: The event detail page opens showing title / date / time / duration / platform and a description.

Steps: On an event detail page (for a free event) click the Register button.
Expected Result: A confirmation message appears. The event now shows as Registered in your list.

Steps: On a paid event click Register. Complete the Stripe payment form that appears.
Expected Result: After payment the event shows as Paid. You receive a confirmation email (if email is configured).

Steps: On the Events page scroll to the Your registrations section.
Expected Result: Events you have registered for are listed with their status (Registered / Paid / Payment pending).

Test Name: Join button appears for registered event
Steps: Open the detail of an event you are registered for where a join link has been set.
Expected Result: A Join button is visible with the correct platform label (e.g. Join on Teams).

Steps: Open the detail of a completed event that has a recording or slides.
Expected Result: You see a recording (YouTube embed) and/or slide deck download links in a Resources section.

Steps: Click Scheduled Meetings in the sidebar.
Expected Result: The meetings page loads showing a calendar or list view.

Steps: Use the Month / Week / Day / List toggle buttons.
Expected Result: The view changes accordingly. Month shows a full calendar grid, List shows a simple list.

Steps: Click the Previous / Next arrows or the Today button on the calendar.
Expected Result: The calendar moves to the correct period. Today returns to the current date.

Steps: Click on a meeting in the calendar or list.
Expected Result: A detail panel opens showing host name / team / date / time / join link.

Steps: Click the Book a meeting button. Select a host from the list.
Expected Result: A calendar booking widget appears for the selected host. You can pick a date and time.

Steps: Type a host name or team name in the search box.
Expected Result: Only matching meetings are shown.

Steps: Use the team filter dropdown to select a specific team.
Expected Result: Only meetings for that team are displayed.

Steps: Click Support Tickets in the sidebar.
Expected Result: The My Tickets page loads showing a stats bar (total / open / pending / closed) and your ticket list.

Steps: Click New Ticket. Fill in subject (3+ characters) and description (10+ characters). Optionally set category and priority. Click Submit.
Expected Result: The ticket appears in your list with status Open.

Steps: Try to submit a ticket with a subject shorter than 3 characters or description shorter than 10.
Expected Result: An error message appears and the ticket is not created.

Steps: Click on a ticket in the list.
Expected Result: The ticket detail page opens showing subject / status / priority / conversation thread.

Steps: On a ticket detail page type a reply in the text area and click Send.
Expected Result: Your reply appears in the conversation thread. The ticket's last updated time changes.

Steps: Click Org Tickets (or the organisation tab).
Expected Result: A list of all tickets across your organisation is displayed (read-only).

Steps: On the tickets page check the stats bar at the top.
Expected Result: You see total / open / pending / closed counts with percentage bars, plus average response and resolution times.

Steps: Click Knowledge Base under Resources in the sidebar.
Expected Result: A list of articles appears with titles and categories.

Steps: Type a keyword in the search box.
Expected Result: Only articles matching the keyword are shown.

Steps: Click a category tab (e.g. Getting started / Reporting / Support).
Expected Result: Only articles in that category are shown. The count badge updates.

Steps: Click on any article in the list.
Expected Result: The full article opens with content / author name / published date.

Steps: While reading an article scroll down to the Related articles section.
Expected Result: Related articles are listed and clickable.

Steps: While reading an article click the Knowledge Base link in the breadcrumb.
Expected Result: You are taken back to the article listing.

Steps: Click Video Library under Resources in the sidebar.
Expected Result: A list of training and recording videos appears with thumbnails.

Steps: Type a keyword in the search box.
Expected Result: Only matching videos are shown.

Steps: Click on any video card.
Expected Result: A lightbox / fullscreen player opens and the video plays.

Steps: Scroll through the video library page.
Expected Result: You see separate sections for Featured, Recent, and Popular videos.

Steps: Click Document Library under Resources in the sidebar.
Expected Result: A list of downloadable documents appears.

Steps: Type a keyword in the search box.
Expected Result: Only matching documents are shown.

Steps: Click on a document or its download button.
Expected Result: The file downloads or opens in a new tab.

Steps: Click Invoices in the sidebar.
Expected Result: A list of your invoices appears with number / status / amount.

Steps: Type an invoice number in the search box.
Expected Result: Only matching invoices are shown.

Steps: Use the status filter (All / Outstanding / Overdue / Paid).
Expected Result: Only invoices with that status are shown.

Steps: Look at the header above the invoice table.
Expected Result: You see total count / overdue count / outstanding count.

Steps: Click the Pay now button on an outstanding or overdue invoice.
Expected Result: You are taken to the Stripe payment flow. After payment the invoice status updates to Paid.

Steps: Click your avatar or Profile in the sidebar.
Expected Result: The profile page loads showing your personal details.

Steps: Change your first name / last name / phone / job title and click Save.
Expected Result: A success message appears and the changes are saved.

Steps: If you have company edit permissions change the company name / domain / phone / address and click Save.
Expected Result: A success message appears and the changes are saved.

Steps: If you do NOT have company edit permissions check the company section.
Expected Result: The company fields are either hidden or displayed as read-only.

Steps: Click each item in the left sidebar one by one.
Expected Result: Each page loads correctly. The active link is highlighted.

Steps: Press Cmd+K (Mac) or Ctrl+K (Windows). Type a page name.
Expected Result: Matching pages appear in the search results. Clicking one navigates to that page.

Steps: Click the bell icon in the top navigation bar.
Expected Result: A dropdown opens showing upcoming events or meetings within the next 7 days.

Steps: If an admin has disabled a feature (e.g. Videos) check the sidebar.
Expected Result: The disabled feature's link is not shown in the sidebar. Navigating directly to its URL redirects you away.

Steps: Click the dark mode toggle (sun/moon icon) in the sidebar.
Expected Result: The portal switches between light and dark themes. All text remains readable.

Steps: Open the portal on a mobile device or resize your browser to a narrow width.
Expected Result: The sidebar collapses to a hamburger menu. All pages remain usable and readable.

Steps: Go to /admin/login.
Expected Result: The admin login form appears with email and password fields.

Steps: Enter valid admin email and password. Click Sign in.
Expected Result: You are redirected to the admin Event Management page.

Steps: Enter a wrong password and click Sign in.
Expected Result: An error message appears. You are NOT logged in.

Steps: Click Event management in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: A table of all events appears with title / type / date / status.

Steps: Use the status filter (All / Upcoming / Completed / Cancelled / Draft / Published).
Expected Result: Only matching events are shown.

Steps: Click Create event. Fill in title / type / start date / duration. Click Save.
Expected Result: The new event appears in the list with status Draft.

Steps: Click on any event in the admin list.
Expected Result: The admin event detail page loads showing all event info and registration list.

Steps: On the event detail page change the title or date and click Save.
Expected Result: The changes are saved and a success toast appears.

Steps: On the event detail page click Send invites. Select a HubSpot contact list and confirm.
Expected Result: Invitations are sent. A success message appears.

Steps: On the event detail page find a registrant and change their status (e.g. mark as Attended).
Expected Result: The registration status updates and a success toast appears.

Steps: On the event detail page click Cancel event and confirm.
Expected Result: The event status changes to Cancelled.

Steps: On the event detail page click Edit on Slides or Recording. Enter a URL and save.
Expected Result: The URL is saved and visible on the public event detail.

Steps: Click Reports in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: The reports page loads with stat cards and an event breakdown table.

Steps: Look at the stat cards at the top of the reports page.
Expected Result: You see Sessions / Time spent / KB views / Tickets metrics for the last 30 days.

Steps: Scroll to the per-event breakdown table.
Expected Result: Each event shows invited / registered / attended / no-show / paid / cancelled counts.

Steps: Click the Export CSV button.
Expected Result: A CSV file is downloaded with all event report data.

Steps: Click the Refresh button.
Expected Result: The data reloads and the stats update.

Steps: Click Ticket stats in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: The ticket stats page loads with 4 stat cards and a category table.

Steps: Check the Live customer tickets stat card.
Expected Result: It shows the count of tickets belonging to customers marked as live/active in HubSpot.

Steps: Check the Active tickets stat card.
Expected Result: It shows the total number of tickets with Open or Pending status.

Steps: Check the Avg response time stat card.
Expected Result: It shows the average time to first agent reply in a readable format (e.g. 2h 15m).

Steps: Scroll down to the Ticket types & volume table.
Expected Result: A table shows each ticket category with its count and a visual bar showing relative volume.

Steps: Click the Refresh button.
Expected Result: The stats reload from HubSpot.

Steps: Click Email configuration in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: The email config page loads with a template selector and preview panel.

Steps: Select a template from the dropdown (e.g. Confirmation).
Expected Result: A rendered preview of the email appears with sample data.

Steps: Change the subject or HTML body of a template and click Save.
Expected Result: The override is saved and the preview updates.

Steps: Click the Delete override button on a customised template.
Expected Result: The template reverts to the system default. The preview updates.

Steps: Click Send test email.
Expected Result: A test email is sent to your admin email address. Check your inbox.

Steps: Click Content management in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: A list of videos / content items appears.

Steps: Click Add video. Fill in title / URL / category and click Save.
Expected Result: The new video appears in the list.

Steps: Click on a video in the list.
Expected Result: A lightbox player opens and the video plays.

Steps: Click User management in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: A table of portal users appears with name / email / status.

Steps: Type a name or email in the search box.
Expected Result: Only matching users are shown.

Steps: Use the status filter (Active / Inactive).
Expected Result: Only users with that status are shown.

Steps: Click Create user. Fill in email / first name / last name. Click Save.
Expected Result: The new user appears in the list.

Steps: Click the actions menu on a user and select Offboard. Enter a reason and confirm.
Expected Result: The user is marked as offboarded.

Steps: Click the actions menu and set access to Temporarily blocked or Permanently blocked.
Expected Result: The user's status updates. They can no longer log in.

Steps: Click System settings in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: The settings page loads with multiple sections (General / Features / Communications / etc.).

Steps: In the Feature toggles section turn off a feature (e.g. Videos). Click Save.
Expected Result: The feature is disabled across the portal. Users no longer see it in the sidebar.

Steps: Change the portal name or support email. Click Save.
Expected Result: The changes are saved. The portal name updates where displayed.

Steps: In the Integrations section click Connect HubSpot and complete the OAuth flow.
Expected Result: HubSpot shows as Connected with a green status indicator.

Steps: In the System safety section toggle Maintenance mode on. Click Save.
Expected Result: An admin banner confirms maintenance mode. Users see a maintenance message.

Steps: Make a change on the settings page but do NOT click Save. Try to navigate away.
Expected Result: An unsaved changes indicator appears.

Steps: Click Notifications in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: The notifications management page loads.

Steps: Fill in level (e.g. Warning) / title / message. Optionally set start and end dates. Click Create.
Expected Result: The notification appears in the list and is shown to all portal users.

Steps: Click Delete on an existing notification. Confirm in the modal.
Expected Result: The notification is removed and no longer shown to users.

Steps: Click HubSpot audit in the admin sidebar.
Expected Result: The audit page loads with a Run scan button.

Steps: Click Run scan.
Expected Result: The scan runs and results appear in a table showing any contact data issues.

Steps: Click Export CSV after a scan.
Expected Result: A CSV file is downloaded with the audit results.

Steps: Click each item in the admin sidebar one by one.
Expected Result: Each admin page loads correctly. The active link is highlighted.

Steps: Click the Back to portal button (or link in the sidebar).
Expected Result: You are taken back to the user-facing portal Dashboard.

Steps: Press Cmd+K (Mac) or Ctrl+K (Windows) in the admin panel. Type a page name.
Expected Result: Matching admin pages appear. Clicking one navigates there.
